<function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">python3 << 'EOF'
import urllib.request
import json

pdb_ids = ['2IB5', '4KGE', '4Q7T', '1KFD', '5JZL', '6AA7', '3ADF', '5WJ2', '3M2

2', '2H5Q', 
           '3MGF', '5Y01', '2VZX', '4CQH', '1YZW', '2CHA', '3KZY', '1AK4', '5P21', '1H6R']

for pid in pdb_ids:
    try:
        url = f'https://data.rcsb.org/rest/v1/core/entry/{pid}'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15)
        d = json

.loads(resp.read())
        title = d.get('struct', {}).get('title', 'N/A')
        print(f"{pid}: {title}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{pid}: ERROR {e}")
EOF
</parameter>
<parameter name="timeout">60</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

Let me continue with the PDB API queries to identify all the proteins, then look up the antibody target and build the gBlock.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="shell">
<parameter name="command">python3 << 'EOF'
import urllib.request
import json

pdb_ids = ['2IB5', '4KGE', '4Q7T', '1KFD', '5JZL', '6AA7', '3ADF', '5WJ2', '3M22', '2H5Q', 
           '3MGF', '5Y01', '2VZX', '4CQH', '1YZW', '2CHA', '3KZY', '1AK4', '5P21', '1H6R']

for pid in pdb_ids:
    try:
        url = f'https://data.rcsb.org/rest/v1/core/entry/{pid}'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15)
        d = json.loads(resp.read())
        title = d.get('struct', {}).get('title', 'N/A')
        print(f"{pid}: {title}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{pid}: ERROR {e}")
EOF
</parameter>
<parameter name="timeout">60</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
